description: Pool Tournament App — group stage round-robin into single elimination knockout

items.heading: Set up environment variables
items.description: Create the local environment variable file and commit a .env.example so any developer cloning the repo knows what to configure. Required variables: MONGODB_URI (MongoDB Atlas mongodb+srv:// connection string), NEXTAUTH_SECRET (random string — generate with `openssl rand -base64 32` — used to sign cookies and JWTs), NEXTAUTH_URL (canonical app URL: http://localhost:3000 for local dev, Vercel deployment URL in production). Google OAuth vars (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET) are out of scope until Google auth is added. Acceptance criteria: app boots locally with .env.local in place; .env.example is committed with placeholder values and a comment explaining each variable; .env.local is gitignored.
steps: Confirm .env.local is listed in .gitignore (add it if missing)
Generate NEXTAUTH_SECRET value with: openssl rand -base64 32
Create .env.example committed to the repo with placeholder values and a comment above each variable explaining what it is and how to obtain it

items.heading: Configure NextAuth.js v5 Credentials provider
items.description: Set up the authentication infrastructure using NextAuth.js v5's built-in Credentials provider. This establishes the session cookie that will later be shared with Google OAuth — no migration needed when Google is added. Includes: installing bcryptjs for password hashing, creating the Mongoose USERS model (fields: email with unique index, passwordHash, createdAt), wiring the Credentials provider into the NextAuth config, and confirming the MongoDB adapter is active. Acceptance criteria: a valid email/password pair produces a NextAuth session cookie; an invalid pair returns an auth error; passwords are never stored in plain text.
steps: Create Mongoose USERS model with fields: email (unique index), passwordHash, createdAt
Add Credentials provider to NextAuth config (auth.ts / NextAuth options) — provider validates email + bcrypt.compare against stored hash
Confirm MongoDB adapter is configured in NextAuth options
Smoke-test: signing in with valid credentials sets a session cookie; invalid credentials return an error

items.heading: Registration and login pages with session cookie
items.description: Build the registration and login UI flows that sit on top of the Credentials provider (PRD-002). Registration: email + password form, server action hashes the password with bcrypt, writes the user to the USERS collection, then calls NextAuth signIn to issue the session cookie, and redirects to /dashboard. Login: email + password form, delegates to NextAuth Credentials provider, redirects to /dashboard on success. Duplicate email on registration must show a generic error (e.g. 'Registration failed — please try again') without revealing the email already exists. The /dashboard route must be protected in proxy.ts so unauthenticated users are redirected to /login.
steps: Create /app/register page and form (email, password fields)
Create server action for registration: validate inputs, bcrypt.hash password, insert into USERS, call signIn('credentials'), redirect to /dashboard
Show generic error on duplicate email — do not reveal the email is taken
Create /app/login page and form (email, password fields)
Create server action for login: call signIn('credentials'), redirect to /dashboard on success, show error on failure
Protect /dashboard in proxy.ts — redirect unauthenticated requests to /login
Create placeholder /app/dashboard page (can be minimal — just confirms the user is logged in)
